name: Popular Bank
description_short: Popular Bank is a full-service regional bank offering checking, savings, credit cards, personal loans, and investment services across the U.S., Puerto Rico, and Virgin Islands.
description_long:
Popular Bank is the U.S. banking subsidiary of Popular, Inc., a publicly traded financial services provider founded in 1893. Popular Bank itself opened in the Bronx, New York in 1961 and has grown to become one of the 50 largest U.S. banks by assets. The organization operates across three primary regions: the mainland United States, Puerto Rico, and the U.S. Virgin Islands, maintaining a century-long heritage of customer-focused banking.

Popular Bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business financial products. On the retail side, they provide checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) with tiered rate structures, money market accounts, and IRAs. They issue eight different credit card options and Mastercard debit cards. In the lending space, they offer personal loans for debt consolidation and credit building, home equity lines of credit, and mortgage services. Their investment division, Popular Investments, provides access to stocks, bonds, mutual funds, annuities, insurance products, and retirement and educational planning services. They also offer online and mobile banking with 24/7 account access and a private banking program called Popular Private Client with concierge services.

Popular Bank distinguishes itself through multi-generational relationship banking, emphasizing long-term financial partnerships rather than transactional interactions. They provide extensive ATM access through 55,000+ Allpoint ATMs worldwide plus their own branch network. Their tiered CD rates reward larger deposits with higher APYs (up to 3.85% APY on $1M+ deposits). They also operate a payment processing platform called Clover for business clients, targeting professional practices and service-based businesses.

As a regional bank with significant scale, Popular Bank serves established banking needs well but operates primarily in three geographic regions. Their strengths lie in comprehensive product offerings and long-standing institutional credibility. The main limitation is geographic concentration outside their core markets, and their rates and products are competitive rather than leading-edge compared to national banks or fintech alternatives. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificates of deposit (CDs) with tiered rate structure
Personal loans for debt consolidation and credit building
Clover payment processing platform for businesses
pros: Access to 55,000+ Allpoint ATMs worldwide plus owned branch network across three regions
Tiered CD rates with APY up to 3.85% for deposits of $1M+, rewarding larger savings
Eight credit card options allowing customers to select based on specific needs
24/7 online and mobile banking access with full account management
Comprehensive product suite from checking through retirement planning under one institution
Popular Private Client program offering concierge banking and VIP services
Over 130 years of institutional history with established market presence
cons: Geographic limitations—primarily serves U.S., Puerto Rico, and Virgin Islands only
Rates are competitive but not leading compared to online-only banks or credit unions
No specific mention of fee structures, minimum balances, or account maintenance costs on homepage
Private Client program likely requires high minimum assets, excluding average consumers
best_for: Customers in New York, New Jersey, South Florida, Puerto Rico, or Virgin Islands seeking comprehensive banking
Multi-generational families wanting long-term banking relationships with one institution
Business owners and professional practices needing integrated banking and payment processing (Clover)
High-net-worth individuals interested in private banking, investments, and wealth management services
diagnosis: Popular Bank is best for customers in its core geographic regions (Northeast U.S., Puerto Rico, Virgin Islands) who value comprehensive, relationship-based banking from an established regional institution. The main caveat is that it operates only in three regional markets and its rates are competitive but not exceptional compared to national online banks, making it less suitable for rate-focused savers or customers outside its service areas.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
